name: Biel - GR 1 Historic Trail Loop
name_original: Puy Fonguera y Ripas Altas desde Biel — circular por la Sierra de Luesia
description: Hike the moderate 8.1-mile circular trail from Biel through Sierra de Luesia, featuring panoramic views from Puy Fonguera and diverse forest
region_intro: Hiking around Biel offers diverse terrain characterized by the foothills of the Pyrenees, including forested hills, river valleys, and distinctive rock formations like the Mallos de Agüero. The region features a mix of challenging ascents and more moderate paths, often following historic routes. This landscape provides varied elevation changes and natural beauty for hikers. Best hiking trails around Biel The most popular hiking route is Puy Moné Loop from Pozo Pígalo, a 10.9 miles (17.6 km) trail that takes 5 hours 23 minutes to complete. This difficult loop offers varied terrain and panoramic views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Biel - GR 1 Historic Trail Loop, a moderate 8.1 miles (13.1 km) path. This route follows a historic trail, showcasing the region's cultural and natural scenery. Local hikers also love the Puy Fonguera - Cabo Bal - Puy Moné — return via the Sierra de Luesia, a 11.0 miles (17.7 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Luesia, often completed in about 5 hours 28 minutes. Hiking around Biel is defined by forested hills, historic paths, and notable rock formations, such as the Mallos de Agüero. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Biel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1,600 hikers have used komoot to explore Biel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Biel?
A: There are over 45 hiking trails around Biel, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. These include 7 easy routes, 28 moderate trails, and 12 more challenging ascents.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Biel?
A: Yes, Biel offers 7 easy hiking trails. These routes are generally shorter and have less elevation gain, making them ideal for a relaxed walk or a family outing. While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed here, the easy trails are a great starting point.

Q: What kind of challenging hikes can I find in the Biel region?
A: For those seeking a challenge, Biel features 12 difficult hiking trails. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33879907" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luesia Castle – Mirador de las Ripas Altas loop from Luesia&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 10.5 miles (16.8 km) with significant elevation changes, offering a demanding yet rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any circular walks available in Biel?
A: Yes, many trails around Biel are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979973485/biel-gr-1-historic-trail-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biel - GR 1 Historic Trail Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.1 miles (13.1 km) circular path that showcases the region's cultural and natural scenery.

Q: What are some notable natural landmarks or viewpoints to see while hiking in Biel?
A: The region is known for its distinctive rock formations. You can explore the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2703458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mallos de Agüero&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/349408" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Cielo - Panoramic View&lt;/a&gt; for breathtaking vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/802386" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz de Escalete&lt;/a&gt; also offers a dramatic natural gorge to discover.

Q: Are there any historic sites or castles to visit near the hiking trails?
A: Absolutely. The area around Biel is rich in history. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5271556" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biel Castle&lt;/a&gt; itself, or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5271588" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luesia Castle and Church of El Salvador&lt;/a&gt;. Many trails, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979973485/biel-gr-1-historic-trail-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biel - GR 1 Historic Trail Loop&lt;/a&gt;, follow historic routes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Biel?
A: The hiking trails in Biel are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the panoramic views, and the opportunity to explore both natural beauty and historic sites like the Mallos de Agüero.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Biel?
A: The region's climate, characterized by the foothills of the Pyrenees, generally makes spring and autumn ideal for hiking. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. While winter hikes are possible, some higher elevation trails might be affected by snow or colder conditions.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by the Mallos de Agüero?
A: Yes, the iconic Mallos de Agüero rock formations are a prominent feature in the region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31814354" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mallos de Agüero – Mallos de Agüero loop from Zerro Sanzerola&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.8 miles (14.2 km) route that specifically takes you through this stunning landscape.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the hiking trails in Biel?
A: While specific cafes directly on trails are not listed, the villages and towns surrounding the hiking areas, such as Biel and Luesia, typically offer local establishments where you can find refreshments and meals after your hike.

Q: What is the typical duration for hikes in Biel?
A: Hikes in Biel vary significantly in duration. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974270706/puy-mone-loop-from-pozo-pigalo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puy Moné Loop from Pozo Pígalo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult trail that takes approximately 5 hours 23 minutes, while shorter, easier routes can be completed in a couple of hours.
tips: This village does not go unnoticed! In its center, you cannot miss its impressive castle. In the plains of the province of Zaragoza, its imposing keep is indeed visible from afar.
The route from Luesia to Puy Fonguera is known for its varied vegetation and stunning landscapes. Furthermore, the area is rich in flora and fauna, making it an ideal destination for nature lovers.
Ripas Altas Viewpoint, a spectacular eroded hillside that we will see from the trail located in the Sierra de Luesia

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Puy Fonguera and Ripas Altas trail?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from the recreational area of the L'Artica refuge. You can reach this point via a paved road that turns into a well-maintained dirt track after about 4.1 kilometers from the town of Luesia.

faqs.question: Is the Puy Fonguera and Ripas Altas trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, dogs are generally welcome on trails in this region, especially in protected landscapes like the Sierra de Santo Domingo. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and for their safety.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike Puy Fonguera and Ripas Altas?
faqs.answer: Given the diverse forests and higher elevations, late spring, summer, and early autumn are ideal. The beech and oak forests would be particularly beautiful in autumn, while summer offers clearer views from the summit. Always check weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for hiking in the Sierra de Luesia?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail, as it is located within the Protected Landscape of the Sierra de Santo Domingo, which is freely accessible for recreational activities.

faqs.question: Is the Puy Fonguera and Ripas Altas trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate. While it involves a significant ascent to Puy Fonguera, making it require a moderate level of physical exertion, hikers with some experience and good fitness should find it manageable. Beginners might find it challenging but rewarding.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a rich variety of landscapes, including beautiful beech and oak forests with abundant holly bushes, charming holm oak groves, and pine forests. As you ascend, the vegetation gives way to expansive pastures. Notable sections include the 'hayedo de la Bal' beech forest and the 'senda del Crebolar' with its holly grove.

faqs.question: What are the main viewpoints and landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the historic Biel Castle at the start, the Puy Fonguera Summit and Viewpoint offering expansive panoramic views with an orientation table, and the scenic Mirador de las Ripas Altas.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Puy Fonguera and Ripas Altas hike?
faqs.answer: This circular hike typically takes around 4 hours and 12 minutes to complete, covering a distance of approximately 13.1 kilometers.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other marked paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route follow parts of other official trails, including the Sendero Histórico: Zaragoza, PR-Z 117 Biel - Sto Domingo, and PR-Z 114. These paths have been restored and marked by the Asociación Cultural Fayanás, Luesia.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or water sources along the trail?
faqs.answer: The starting point is near the L'Artica refuge, but explicit water sources or facilities directly on the trail are not mentioned. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks for the duration of the hike.

faqs.question: What should I pack for the Puy Fonguera and Ripas Altas hike?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and varied terrain, pack sturdy hiking boots, layers of clothing suitable for changing weather, plenty of water, snacks, a map or GPS device, and sun protection. Binoculars would enhance the panoramic views from Puy Fonguera.

faqs.question: Is this a circular route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Puy Fonguera and Ripas Altas from Biel is a circular route, meaning you will end your hike at the same point where you started, offering a diverse experience without retracing your steps.
